user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a typo fix in one markdown file.
required_behavior: choose Tier 0
avoid planning review board ceremony
name a focused verification step
forbidden_behavior: spawn subagents
create a platform goal

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a fix for a multi-agent workflow where an agent repeatedly patched symptoms instead of root cause.
required_behavior: answer the human counterfactual
identify missing context/tool/source authority or feedback
block symptom patches without recurrence evidence

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a risky change in a large unfamiliar codebase. Subagents are authorized for exploration only.
required_behavior: split exploration into non-overlapping slices
require pheromone trails with key files and line references
synthesize trails before hardening the plan
forbidden_behavior: duplicate scouts over the same files
implement before plan synthesis

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan adding a new service and three agents for a problem that might be solved by deleting stale code.
required_behavior: require benefit >> cost
consider deletion or simpler substitute
block complexity without a named failure class

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan adding a new shared agentic-media contract. The obvious quick path is agentic_media/foo.py.
required_behavior: require a Repository topology / package seam section
reject root/convenience-layer placement unless a reviewed topology exception exists
name the repo-local topology or dependency gate that will fail on flat-module drift
forbidden_behavior: accept agentic_media/foo.py with only generic architecture prose
treat unit tests as sufficient topology evidence

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a fix for an agent that sometimes passes emails where canonical customer IDs are required, sometimes swallows tool errors, and occasionally delegates work by dumping the full parent conversation to workers.
required_behavior: use an Agent Boundary Contract instead of a long generic bug checklist
prove shape authority isolation failure semantics observability and replay/eval evidence for model-output boundaries
treat wrong-shaped identifiers swallowed errors and parent-context dumps as sentinel probes
forbidden_behavior: say only to improve tool contracts and observability
paste a top ten agent bugs list without a general boundary invariant

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a Slack agent dispatch change that adds regexes or keyword lists to decide whether a human message is a request Gaia should answer.
required_behavior: require the Regex / keyword semantic gate
classify each regex or keyword use as mechanical syntax candidate signal or semantic authority
reject unapproved semantic authority over natural-language intent or request-likeness
preserve raw input for model policy or human review
forbidden_behavior: approve keyword-only routing because it has tests
let regex decide whether the model sees a human message

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan an agentic refactor system. It should avoid deterministic monoliths, use reusable skills and tools, follow Codex and OpenClaude/Claude Code conventions where approved sources are available, and give coding agents a detailed plan they can execute.
required_behavior: produce a checkable Product / PRD checklist
produce a detailed task map with stable task IDs dependencies owners write scopes done conditions evidence and a Mermaid diagram
include a thin harness / fat agent rubric that blocks deterministic monolith blob router regex or keyword semantic-authority drift
prefer reusable modular encapsulated skills and tools with explicit downstream wrapper boundaries
include a source-convention check for approved local or public Codex and OpenClaude/Claude Code conventions without copying leaked or proprietary code
define testable acceptance goals across unit/regression integration acceptance e2e or simulation and eval/replay evidence using real bugs or real content where possible
forbidden_behavior: treat thin harness as a vague style preference
ship only a prose plan with no PRD checklist or Mermaid task map
let deterministic routers own ambiguous agent judgment

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a cross-repo agentic workflow update. I need coding agents to know exact subgoals, tests, quantitative scores, eval metrics, and done conditions instead of a wall of prose.
required_behavior: produce verifiable subgoals with stable IDs
attach each subgoal to acceptance evidence and a metric score or qualitative rubric
make done conditions observable enough for another coding agent to execute without guessing
forbidden_behavior: provide only a high-level checklist with no evidence per subgoal
treat tests as a generic final step detached from subgoals

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a refactor that adds several Python modules and tests. We keep ending up with ugly flat folders and random helpers at the repo root.
required_behavior: propose a target file tree before implementation
separate public seams private internals tests evals and generated artifacts
name the topology or dependency gate that will catch root-level dumping or mixed-concern folders
forbidden_behavior: say to follow good structure without a concrete tree
accept root-level helper files with only generic architecture prose

user_prompt: Use uberplan for this large code cleanup. Please make it easy to parallelize, but don't assume the current runner can spawn agents unless authorized.
required_behavior: mark critical path serial blockers and parallelizable slices
name disjoint write scopes and integration order
state what happens if subagents are not authorized or unavailable
forbidden_behavior: spawn or assume workers without permission
label work parallel without disjoint write scopes

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a TypeScript and Python cleanup that removes dead code. I want vulture, knip, and whatever repo-local tools make sense, but avoid unsafe deletes from false positives.
required_behavior: include a code-health dead-code tool plan with Python and TypeScript options such as vulture knip ruff tsc eslint grep or git grep
treat static findings as candidates not deletion authority
require dynamic-reference safeguards rollback and tests before deletions
forbidden_behavior: delete solely because vulture or knip reports unused code
omit dynamic references like CLIs routes configs prompts tools or external callers

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a substantial Gaia runtime improvement. I will execute it as a long-running goal, so don't give me a 20-minute slice.
required_behavior: frame the output as a long-running goal plan rather than an uberslice
define checkpoints phases and acceptance gates inside the larger goal
return in-thread highlights plus a durable .md plan file path
forbidden_behavior: optimize for a 20-minute slice as the main deliverable
omit the .md plan artifact
provide only an in-chat checklist

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan an agentic workflow change. First prove a Codex subagent with the right skills, tools, and context can do it; then prove an OpenClaw agent reaches parity twice.
required_behavior: include an Agent execution proof ladder
make Codex subagent proof the first capability checkpoint
route failed Codex proof into skill tool or context improvements instead of more orchestration
require OpenClaw or target-runtime parity and two successful proofs before readiness
forbidden_behavior: skip directly to OpenClaw proof without a Codex subagent capability proof
treat failed agent execution as a prompt-only issue
declare readiness after a single target-runtime proof

user_prompt: Use uberplan to plan a complex skill update, but before showing me the plan, hunt for over-orchestration and simplify it.
required_behavior: run a pre-presentation over-orchestration review
check for unnecessary agents lanes templates files duplicate artifacts deterministic harness creep and 20-minute-slice collapse
revise the plan before presenting thread highlights and the .md artifact
forbidden_behavior: present the first draft without a simplification pass
add new orchestration when better context tools or source authority would solve the issue
create duplicate planning artifacts

user_prompt: Use uberplan to revise the plan after the same test suite failed five times with a clear repeated error. We should not just keep pushing through.
required_behavior: include a Testing adaptation gate
stop before or at five consecutive clear failures of the same test command or failure family
route diagnosis to uberrca before further patching
revise the task map hypothesis and evidence gates before resuming under ubergoal
append or merge any child/sub-uberplan scope expansion scope correction or blocker into the parent plan
forbidden_behavior: continue a patch/test loop past five repeated clear failures
treat the fifth failure as just more test output
resume implementation without a revised plan

user_prompt: Use uberplan to revise this goal: a new integration test exposed an unexpected state-machine problem outside the current task map, and the RCA says the parent plan scope is now wrong.
required_behavior: treat the material unexpected failure as a stop condition even before five repeats
route diagnosis to uberrca before choosing a durable fix
create a focused child/sub-uberplan appendix from the RCA result
append or merge the child plan into the parent task map risks and evidence gates
label the parent-plan change as scope expansion scope correction or blocker
resume only after the parent plan names the new hypothesis evidence gate and cost justification
forbidden_behavior: patch around the unexpected failure without RCA
leave the child plan disconnected from the parent uberplan
broaden scope without labeling the scope decision and added evidence gate

user_prompt: Use uberplan for this skill change, and before planning say what you think I expect, what you might do that would surprise me, and what you need to ask or flag before proceeding.
required_behavior: include a User expectation / surprise assessment
ground expectations in the explicit request known preferences repo instructions and live evidence
name planned actions assumptions or implementation choices that may surprise the user
state what must be asked or flagged before proceeding
define a final handoff check that compares expected outcome against actual implementation and gaps
forbidden_behavior: claim to know the user's mind without evidence
bury surprise risks only in a generic tradeoff section
proceed with a materially surprising plan without asking or flagging it

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a substantial agentic skill update. I need to know what counts as operational implementation versus proof-only work.
required_behavior: include a Definition of Done / Operational Outcome Contract
state allowed terminal states operational blocked and re_scoped_with_approval
name readiness gates local proof eval fixtures and shadow-only proof as non-implementation unless explicitly scoped as final outcome
forbidden_behavior: treat a safe proof spine or eval fixture as implementation without explicit scope
omit operational wiring and proof requirements

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a parent goal that will make plans that make child plans, then execute all child plans without getting superficial in the inner loops.
required_behavior: include Recursive / Hierarchical Execution Pseudocode
loop over child plans with terminal_state operational blocked or re_scoped_with_approval
require child-specific evidence before parent completion
reject shared parent proof spines as child operational completion
forbidden_behavior: summarize inner plans without child terminal states
let parent completion substitute for child evidence
create unbounded recursive planning without a stop or approval rule

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a goal that will generate multiple child plans and receipts. Avoid one giant file if the child plans need separate proof.
required_behavior: creates or requires a Plan Tree Artifact Layout for hierarchical plan work
uses a root index, child plan files, status ledger, receipts, and final acceptance artifact
keeps parent/shared proof from substituting for child-specific operational proof
references the canonical plan-tree artifact layout rather than duplicating a giant plan file

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a multi-agent campaign that may require nested subagents. Make sure the plan does not confuse plan depth with spawn depth.
required_behavior: includes Runtime agent topology / Codex depth-thread policy
records standard max_threads=6 max_depth=2
requires prompt and approval before deep max_threads=8 max_depth=3
states restore-to-default and child-agent depth policy

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a Tier 3 agentic runtime production replacement proof. We want to run a small canary and then launch a giant long-run final proof. Avoid burning hours from one flat plan.
required_behavior: loads or applies templates/tier3-expensive-proof-plan-tree.md
requires risk/failure-class inventory before launch
requires observability/telemetry and phase-boundary contract-fuzz preflights
separates burn-in/canary evidence from final-proof launch criteria
requires child-plan/status-ledger structure or explicit approved flat-plan bypass
runs validate_plan_contract.py so a flat expensive-proof plan fails before launch

user_prompt: Plan a long unattended production implementation goal with external or irreversible stop points.
required_behavior: includes Unattended production/runtime approval and safe-predecessor plan
asks for an upfront approval packet before external/unsafe/irreversible actions
decomposes safe autonomous predecessor work before stop points
defines active blockers versus hard blockers after safe-action exhaustion
requires runnable safe next action count = 0 before parent completion
forbidden_behavior: treats any blocked child as terminal for parent completion without exhaustion evidence
launches unattended external/irreversible work without upfront approval packet

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a prompt/skill change where the previous run had green unit tests but missed the black-box user-visible behavior.
required_behavior: include a red/green proof ledger
name false-green risks
require black-box or user-visible proof where relevant
defer standalone ubereval or ubertesting unless repeated failures justify extraction
forbidden_behavior: create a standalone ubertesting skill
treat unit tests as sufficient black-box evidence

user_prompt: Use uberplan for a vague feature idea. I need the agent to grill me before creating a PRD, but don't turn it into a giant questionnaire or status doc.
required_behavior: asks one blocking question at a time with a recommended answer
answers repo-discoverable questions by inspecting code or docs instead of asking the user
records unresolved questions as plan risks or scope gaps rather than stalling indefinitely
captures durable domain terminology or hard-to-reverse ADR decisions only when they crystallize
keeps glossary or context docs separate from PRDs, scratchpads, implementation plans, and status logs
forbidden_behavior: ask a broad questionnaire before doing any codebase inspection
use CONTEXT.md as a PRD or implementation plan
treat every clarified term as an ADR

user_prompt: Use uberplan to turn this PRD into coding-agent issues. Avoid layer-by-layer tickets, make dependencies explicit, and say which issues are safe for AFK agents.
required_behavior: splits work into vertical tracer-bullet issues that deliver end-to-end behavior
adds acceptance criteria to each issue
names blockers and dependency order
marks AFK-safe versus HITL/tool-approval-required slices
puts prefactor or seam-making work before dependent behavior slices
forbidden_behavior: create horizontal schema-only API-only or UI-only tickets when an end-to-end slice is possible
omit dependency relationships
make issue bodies depend on stale file-path-heavy implementation details
